decision: Implement SPIFFE X.509-SVID issuance/rotation/mount lifecycle as portable logic in package internal/spire (new files svid.go, issuer.go, manager.go), layered on the existing workload-registration substrate (service.go) and reusing config.SPIREConfig and the D025 SPIFFE ID format.
rationale: Issue #17 (per-microVM cryptographic identity) requires SVID acquisition, 0400 mount at /var/run/secrets/spiffe/svid.json, TTL enforcement, and rotation-before-expiry. On main, internal/spire/service.go already registers SPIRE workload ENTRIES and internal/firecracker/vsock_proxy.go bridges guest->host SPIRE agent, and docs/specs/spiffe-integration-status.md lists the SVID CLIENT as the remaining work. #17 is therefore the issuance/rotation/mount LAYER on top of existing registration, not a greenfield SPIFFE build. Building in package spire (not a new top-level package) reuses BuildSPIFFEID/SPIREConfig and avoids a parallel ID scheme; it has zero file overlap with open PR #137 (internal/credisolation/, cmd/nanofuse/isolation*.go) and PR #120.
alternatives: New top-level package internal/svid: rejected, would fork the SPIFFE-ID/config conventions already in package spire. Adding go-spiffe/v2 as a dependency now: deferred, because the real Workload-API acquisition runs guest-side over the vsock proxy and cannot be exercised on a dev box; a provider-agnostic Source seam keeps go.mod/go.sum and act CI light while documenting go-spiffe workloadapi.X509Source as the production Source. Host-side SVID injection into the guest fs: rejected, contradicts the committed guest-client->vsock-proxy->SPIRE-agent topology.

decision: Ship the testable lifecycle core (acquire -> atomic 0400 write -> rotate 15m-before-expiry -> fail-safe naming SPIRE unreachability) with an injectable clock and a local-CA dev/test Source; explicitly DEFER to runtime: mounting inside the microVM guest fs, aborting VM start on issuance failure, and the literal 45-minute rotation integration test.
rationale: Prior closed PR30 (decision auth-001) was rejected for unwired rotation semantics that did not integrate with shutdown cancellation or actual issuance. This implementation makes rotation context-cancellable, drives correctness via a deterministic fake clock (no real 45m wait, no sleep-flake), and verifies genuine crypto (per-VM distinct SPIFFE ID, URI-SAN==SPIFFE-ID, signature verifies against the trust bundle, new cert valid before old expires). Runtime-only behaviors are documented as deferred rather than fabricated as passing.
alternatives: Real SPIRE agent + 45m wall-clock test: impossible on dev box and flaky. Wall-clock short-TTL loop test: rejected as -race flaky; fake clock chosen instead.
